mapping_note: Legacy Halcyon reviews mapped to active Halcyon UIEM and CIEM product handles.
title: Good Sound / Bad Fit
body:
First of all, I am using Canary, a flagship product of the same AAW company..
I feel very high performance for the price, so I am very satisfied with the sound performance.

However, as a product that is worn only as an audio device, I think the fit is also an important factor.

This design will be very much like and dislike to Asians, and it is more of a fit factor than an aesthetic part.

I have introduced it to many people and almost all of them complained of having pain.

No matter how good the product is, listening to music with pain is not an easy task.

It is unfortunate that good products set standards only for European and British Americans.

target_product_title: A3H+ Lux Edition Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: Quite impressed
body: It's been days testing the AAW A3H+ with my old but reliable Lune MKV cable and I'm impressed. Before the A3H (and now the A3H+), I had the Ultimate Ears TripleFi 10Pro and ended up missing the powerful bass from the UETF10P when I stepped up to the A3H but ended up liking the A3H because it was a massive improvement in every other area. That is...until I got the A3H+. This one improves upon the A3H in the mids and lows massively. It's clearer and the bass is definitely way more than I expected. I even had to lower the bass a bit using an Equalizer because it's that much more powerful than A3H's. It'll take a while getting used to these improvements but I do not regret at all getting the A3H+. I now coupled the A3H with the Nyx BT5 cable. Using both units with SpinFit CP145 eartips. I highly recommend it.

target_product_title: Z05 AuAg Launch Edition
mapping_note: Legacy M5-5D mapped to Z05 AuAg per AAW request.
title: Clean and balanced Sound with Hefty Bass
body:
I only have had the M5-5Ds for about a week so this writing is more of a quick impression rather than review. 

Sound Quality:
Bass and the soundstage are the first things I have noticed. The bass is deep and punchy. Although they sometimes sounded a little loose at very low frequency. The soundstage is not particularly wide but the layering and separation are quite impressive.

The mids sounded quite natural and balanced. They are not recessed. The treble is clear and smooth. Although I wish there could be a little more sparkles.

The fit:
The shells are on the larger side. They do fit comfortably but may cause sore spots after a couple of hours. 

Built Quality & Packaging:
The M5-5Ds feel very solid. The cables (2 of them) came with the earphones have good quality. Six pairs of ear tips were included. But I got better fit with Spinfit tips. I also like the large leather storage box.

Additional thoughts:
I would only give 4-star if these were sold at the suggested retail price. But with the significant discount from Null Audio, these M5-5Ds provided great value for the money.

target_product_title: Z05 AuAg Launch Edition
mapping_note: Legacy AXH mapped to Z05 AuAg as the successor product per AAW request.
title: Great Workmanship & Nice Sound
body:
Treble：clear and comfortable, better than my last UE TF10
Mid：I think it's AXH's selling point. Leaning forward and smooth. TF10 hid the mid and now they spring out beautifully.
Bass：A little thin and doesn't go deep, but still has its quality. Perhaps it would be better after changing the cable to a UPOCC.

Overall：5 STARS for pops, jazz, classic and ACG, but 4 STARS for rocks, heavy metal.The separation is excellent and sound stage is 3D-like (with 4.4 balanced FOB&my WM1Z). This is a gorgeous IEM with high cost-performance ratio and workmanship.If you like crystal treble and mid then it won't let you down~

target_product_title: A3H+ Lux Edition Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: No more universal fit for me.
body:
This is my first CIEM and I’m truly impressed with the A3H. I think gone are the days of me buying the off-the-shelf universal fits. The fit is nice, its got a little bit of pressure the ears which enables the noise isolation. But what has me impressed is the sound quality. 
 
I’m no audiophile but with the A3H its hard not to become one as it brings out every little nuance in the music, sounds that I have never noticed in my favourite tracks like the vinyl scratches on sample and the hidden bass notes before the massive drops. And boy are those bass drops massive. I’m sucker for bass and these puppies deliver. My commute to work feels like I’m standing next to the speakers at concerts. Having the luxury of not needing to drive to work does mean not having access to my car’s subs and amps to get that good ole car bass. These fill out that void for me and then some. Its got that buttery yet punch bass that you feel rather than hear, its is fantastic. 
 
If you’re out not the market for nice in-ears, go custom and if you do, I highly recommend the A3H, no regrets.

title: Balanced & clear! Guitar folks will rejoice on these!
body:
Review for AAW M20 & LUNE SERIES MKIII cable. This is an upgrade from the stock cable on my AAW M20. It is clear & easy to wear as it's light are tangle-free. The soundstage is better now and the mids is now clear.Used & abused it everyday from listening to music & tracking guitars during recording sessions. Lately the plug is abit loose & the became 
intermittent. They have repaired it free of charge as it's still under warranty. Lately I dropped the IEM too and it broke. They repaired it with a minimum charge, due to negligence btw, abusing it. lol Suggesting that it should be in unbreakable enamel shell next time for live music purposes. :D Awesome gears Null Audio! Rock on! \m/

target_product_title: A3H+ Lux Edition Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: Good Experience
body:
So I decided to create my first CIEMs that sounds good and falls withing my budget range. I then visited them to try out the the A1D, A2H pro, and the A3H Pro.
My choice falls to the A3H Pro.

The one who attended to create my ear impressions was really friendly, but because there was only one person he was quite busy.
moving to choose the design, because there were other customers he didn't explain into details.
I chose the color of the faceplate and the body..
As I though the logo has an additional fee, I decided to choose the 'No Logo' option to keep the price minimum.

So for you guys who want's to create one with them, let me inform you that the standard logo is free of charge :D

I was quoted 6 weeks fo my unit, but got it at the start of week 5.

moving to the A3H Pro, they seem to have a new design for the packaging and the accessories.
First the cable, they provide a new designed cable different from the ones you might see online.
They are transparent white with a 'matte' feel.
also quite thinner than the previous one but feels high quality though..

the carrying case is a small square hardcover similar to those beat iems ones, instead of the pelican case type that you may see previously on the reviews of AAW products.

the fit is excellent, but as this is my first customs I have a problem on inserting them into my ears.

In the end I might say that I am satisfied with the service and product.

oh, they are also quick on replying their emails!

target_product_title: Mockingbird Reference Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Legacy W500 mapped to current Mockingbird UIEM and CIEM per AAW request.
title: Gradient multi color schemed W500
body:
Super Happy with the result. Great Sound. Great Finish. 
 
The Good:I have been looking for a set of high end monitors that are not simply flat or too analytical. These deliver all that I have been looking for and so much more, in the sense that it offers the exciting sound that I was looking for but could not find for so long. The sound is very clean but you still have a nice bottom end bump while maintaining accuracy. 
 
The Not so good: I do wish however that there was some grippy section or indentation to make removal easier. The cable while good tend to be a bit sticky and should really be about 5 inches longer. The final bad thing about these is that weird people would want to touch it simply because it is different and then simply dump it back on your desk if you are not careful...treating it like your regular I-buds, so it would be best not to let people touch it. 
 
Happy customer for sure :) 
https://www.facebook.com/acousticwerkes/photos/pcb.574295022773281/574294662773317/?type=3&theater

target_product_title: Mockingbird Reference Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Live AAW W900 handle is now Mockingbird custom; duplicated to Mockingbird UIEM and CIEM.
title: AAW900, an elegant musical reference
body:
I really like this iem, and I have high expectations (I own SE5 Ultimate, KSE1500, S-EM9, Prophile 8. etc.) and I was not disappointed. 

It is airy and spacious, very detailed, and musical without being too colored. What is particlulary impressive, is that besides have 9BA + 1dynamic, they sound very coherent. The bass is very punchy but tight, controlled and detailed. Mids are clear and even, high are extended yet completely smooth and non peaky. Some could think the highs are not so sparkly but I like this sound, it is not fatiguing yet technical. 

All in all, if you look for a great reference all rounder, you should consider these.

I really like this iem, and I have high expectations (I own SE5 Ultimate, KSE1500, S-EM9, Prophile 8. etc.) and I was not disappointed. 
 
It is airy and spacious, very detailed, and musical without being too colored. What is particlulary impressive, is that besides have 9BA + 1dynamic, they sound very coherent. The bass is very punchy but tight, controlled and detailed. Mids are clear and even, high are extended yet completely smooth and non peaky. Some could think the highs are not so sparkly but I like this sound, it is not fatiguing yet technical. 
 
All in all, if you look for a great reference all rounder, you should consider these.

target_product_title: Mockingbird Reference Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Live AAW W900 handle is now Mockingbird custom; duplicated to Mockingbird UIEM and CIEM.
title: Brilliant piece of musical masterpiece
body: To start off, i am not a advanced reviewer who can describe a piece of equipment in detail technical aspect. But i would like to share my experience of my W900. i was auditioning W500 back in Nov and really like the bass it was delivering. I then sent a msg to Kevin and realized that W900 will be launching and he was asking if i would like to hold on for a while. When i auditioned the W900 for the first time, i didnt find it as intriguing as W500. The bass didnt seem as impressive as the W500. In fact it sounded a little flat to my taste as i was looking for a V-shape sound signature. However, i noticed that the W900 was a very revealing piece of iem. I could suddenly hear a lot of details that i did not hear before. Essentially it was like upgrading from full HD to 4K. I therefore made the leap and ordered a pair. Now, I am getting hooked to W900 as i am rediscovering all the albums that i have with even more details that i have never heard before. As for the build, they were very well built with no bubbles or flaws. i have requested for custom artwork for my order and they look lovely when i got them. Although there was some fitting issues but it was resolved promptly and now i have perfect fit and sealage. Fantastic job to the guys at AAW.

title: Excellent hybrid reference monitor done right
body:
The W900 a hybrid reference monitor with good layering, imaging and instrument separation. It is easy to pick up alot of details and instrument positions with this monitor. 

The mids have enough body without being overly forward or aggressive, making vocals a pleasure to listen to.

The bass is another strong trait of the W900, the strong, impactful bass kicks in at the right time and moment with great control. 

For years, I have been more accustomed to a warmer/fuller sound signature such as the fitears/westone monitors, but owning a W900 made me appreciate what a hybrid reference monitor can do; bringing out immense amount of details in an exciting manner.

target_product_title: Mockingbird Reference Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Legacy W500 AHMorph mapped to current Mockingbird UIEM and CIEM per AAW request.
title: After 150hrs review
body:
The sound comes to life and it's very comfortable in the ears, a really unique and promising warm sound signature with its balanced treble energy, quite good layering ability. 
Its black background and low frequency naturalness; the craftsmanship and comfort is top notch. 
The W500 AHMorph has the addictive bass and it gives W500 a bit of its warm tonality and plus the wide soundstage, 
till tend to forget that it was there because you to immersed in the great eargasmic experience. 
Oh, and the switch. I'm glad to choice AHMorph. It's more fun than W500. I preferences the tune between Blue and Yellow point. In this tune,(Compare with the Mix tune yellow point) it bring the vocal closer, more deep soundstage, more prominent and slightly more natural hits.

target_product_title: A3H+ Lux Edition Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: Great sound, smooth custom fitting process for a first-time CIEM buyer
body:
Overall buying process: 
- Easy, convenient molding process at Pioneer Junction. The most 'difficult' part is choosing your faceplates, if you can't make up your mind like me. 
- Responsive sales support as well, via email/SMS 
- Took about 6.5 weeks for assembly 
 
Sound: 
- Versatile bass : can be focused, or a sort of rumbly ambient thump, as needed. In fact, the effect of the bass can sometimes be a nice surprise in a musical sense. Other headphones I've tried (DT800, Crossfade M100, B&O H6) don't have such a clear separation between bass and the midrange (IMHO) and articulation. 
- Mids/Highs also much more hard-hitting & articulate than the universal fit A3H I tried at the showroom 
 
Fit/Finish: 
- Comfortable, easy to wear 
- Body is like a polished pebble, but one of the ports is chipped :( 
 
Minor quirks: 
- Case is slightly cumbersome as a portable case, because the lid is separate from the container, which can lead to some fumbling. 
- Wire loops on the top of the ear can occasionally dig into ears, if cable is tugged. Think it's thinner than other ear loops I've worn (e.g. sporty headphones), so can be minorly painful. 
 
Overall - superb sound and happy with my purchase.

mapping_note: Legacy W500 mapped to current Mockingbird UIEM and CIEM per AAW request.
title: W500 Bang for Buck!
body:
This is my first CIEM, and would probably be the last. 
Paired with Fiio x7 Mark II 
 
I would definitely recommend this IEM if you’re into treble, clarity and brightness with a significant depth of soundstage. This IEM is more focus on the higher fq rather than lower, the bass is just right without engulfing the highs of the music. I placed the ordered with the Lune cable hence pairing it with the best economical combination available from Null Audio and have not regretted my choice since. 
 
I was service by ZX at the studio and she was extremely nice! Fitting done by her was extremely good and comfortable, didn’t had to go back for a refit. If you’re new into CIEM and don’t mind cost, it will be an good investment for the next few years. 
 
I would like to send my appreciation to ZX for assisting my friend in reshelling her CIEM too! =)

title: earphonia.com - review - Extended Aural Immersion
body:
Technically this is strong at all aspects but somehow all the musicality is still present, many other technically strong earphones seem to result in a clinical feel. 
With the W900 you get a smoothness, even in all the complexity, the signature is so very well balanced across the whole spectrum.

As I’m sure you can tell I really am impressed with the W900 and it has the perfect balance between musicality and reference. 

Sound stage is so very expansive and the signature coherence really gives a level of refinement, placing the W900 in amongst the top tier league of high end earphones.

target_product_title: Z05 AuAg Launch Edition
mapping_note: Legacy AXH custom mapped to Z05 AuAg as the successor product per AAW request.
title: Sounds different from what I auditioned, but still great.
body:
I believe I may have auditioned a pair which had the original treble focused signature (also noticed by another user on reddit). I actually liked the treble signature. The new signature is more neutral which isn’t a bad thing, but the Lune cable included does not follow in the same spirit. 

With the Lune mk5 cable, lows and highs are elevated, making the mids and vocals sound slightly recessed. The weight of Lune cable also tugs at your ears while you walk which is annoying. With the Arete cable, it brings everything to a neutral position, making AXH a lot more listenable and enjoyable at all volume levels. I’m starting to enjoy the new AXH with Arete and I suggest all to try out the different cables to see which suits your preference. :)

Worksmanship of AAW is impeccable. My CIEMs came looking great, but suffered from a poor fit probably due to the impressions, but there is a good refit policy to support so that’s fine.
location: Singapore, Central Singapore Community Development Council, Singapore

target_product_title: Z05 AuAg Launch Edition
mapping_note: Legacy AXH mapped to Z05 AuAg as the successor product per AAW request.
title: AXH Universal
body: I ordered the AXH Universal Fit IEMs, and after a few weeks of listening can say that I am happy with my purchase. As this is my first pair of hybrid IEMs it is difficult for me to compare them with others. However, I can tell that even though there is a V shape tuning, it is subtle enough to allow for listening to a variety of audio/music (ie. adequate bass without sacrificing clarity in vocals, etc). The largest tips even fit my cavernous ear canals better than I expected. I wish that they would have come with a more compact but study carrying case. The one it shipped in is good for storage or touring, but too large for very portable use. Overall, I am very happy with these!

target_product_title: Z05 AuAg Launch Edition
mapping_note: Legacy AXH mapped to Z05 AuAg as the successor product per AAW request.
title: Neutral and comfortable hybrid iem
body: For me AAW AXH is a good implementation of hybrid iem which plays very relaxed and neutral sound: in 2019 version I didn't hear any mid-hi peaks other users were writing about, at least this is true for my Yulong Aquila and Chord Mojo, although this neutrality, silky mids and deep, layered but rather soft bass may not appeal to some (sometimes - even myself included), so if you want more aggressive sound then I suggest to check other AAW models as well before you buy (e.g. ASH sounded a much more detailed to me). Isolation of universal model is good - of course it's worse than custom, but it blocks most of the external noises when music is playing, so this is a good choice for commuting.

target_product_title: A3H+ Lux Edition Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: Soundstage, Punch, Fit, Warmth and Clarity
body: Now in my 70's I've traded my audiophile ways in for the convenience of MP3s and flac files played on MP3 players... I realize there's a dropoff in musical info and I can hear the difference, but the convenience takes precedence, so I try to make up for it with using quality players such as Cowon Plenue players and IEMs such as the old Audeo PFE 232 and what had been my most recent phones, the Shure SE535.  Having never been satisfied with the Shures but so regretful the Audeos are no longer made, I decided to finally try something from Null.....  The A3H has been amazing.....  My reaction to its greatest attributes are in the title, SOUNDSTAGE, PUNCH, FIT, WARMTH, AND CLARITY.  Soundstage is what hit me first.... Not since my electrostatic days have I been able to experience such a defined soundstage...  I'm hearing instruments and their location on the stage like I've not heard before, and discovering instruments I had forgotten were there when I listen to my old standby teest songs from the audiophile days... it's quite startling to hear even from an MP3.  Of course the info has to be there in the first place but if it's on the MP3, you'll hear it like never before thru these....  And though I'm not really a bass centric listener as so many are (I concentrate on midrange where voices live), it's amazing how the A3H brings out the punch in drum lines and bass lines without overemphasizing...  AS far as  fit, I suspect what the A3H does for fit is not unusual for many, but when compared to the way the Shures fit in my ear, I was surprised at how easily these seal while lying flat in the ear....  Love it!  And as far as my midrange, where there's where I experience the warmth of vocals and clarity is across the board....  I couldnt' be happier with my purchase... One other attribute I've discovered that differentiates these from my prior phones...  It's extraordinarily exciting to hear just how much better these sound the louder you play them... That might sound ...

Solid 5 stars for this outstanding monitor!

target_product_title: POLA Electrostatic Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Only a live POLA universal product exists; not duplicated to custom.
title: Wonderful IEM with new electrostatic technology
body:
In my opinion, POLA has the following characteristics:

1. Well-controlled and detailed treble which is not irritating.
2. Smooth and sentimental vocal, when other IEMs with BA will have occasional presence of sibilance but that is not the case for POLA.
3. Deep and booming bass, that is the most brilliant part of POLA and I can really hear very deep and layered bass as described.

Moreover, a powerful DAP or portable amplifier is needed to drive this giant and a certain run-in period will definitely boost its performance to another level.
location: Central, Central and Western District, Hong Kong

target_product_title: ASH Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: CHEATERS - Poor product, poor service, didnot come with all accessories
body:
I ordered B-stock ASH & AXH IEMs at 30% discount, which has an MSRP of USD 799 & USD 449 respectively. 
1.) While switching the ear tips on the very first day, the stainless mesh on ASH came out. Instead of replacing the IEM piece, they initially offered sending a few stainless meshes, which never arrived.
2.) During A-B comparison, I was astonished to find that there was "absolutely no difference" in the soundstage of ASH (13mm Graphene Driver + 6 x Balanced Armature Driver with Symphonym Tiburon Cable) and AXH (10mm Dynamic Driver + 4 x Balanced Armature Driver with Null Audio Lune Series Detachable Cable).
3.) I then compared with my 20 years old $100 Sony MDR-7506. Apart from lower volume due to higher (63) impedance, again there was no difference.
4.) To be completely unbiased & rule out issues with my hearings, I did blind test with my two daughters. They both have undergone over a decade of Classical Vocal training, play flute, keyboard, and listen to various genre of music like Jazz, Hip hop, K-Pop, Pop, Blues, Rock, Symphonies, etc. Even they couldn't find any significant difference among the 3 IEMs/ headphones. 
5.) When I requested to return one of the IEMs, they refused to accept returns inspite of their 14 days return policy. 
6.) The 2 IEMs did not come with the Flight or 1/4" adapter, as it is mentioned on their site. When I asked for it, they politely agreed but never fulfilled their promise inspite of 4-5 reminders.

I definitely Do Not recommend their product or their service. Just a bunch of liars & sweet talkers.
reply:
It is explicitly stated on the B stock sales page All sales are final, no return or exchange is accepted. 
We are sorry to hear that you could not hear any difference between the multiple IEM and headphones, however, that is not our problem.
location: New York, New York, United States

body:
I really like these.  They fit really well and look great, I went with the sort of rock/gemstone look.  The sound is good and I can wear them for a while.  They sound pretty similar to the UE5 pros but warmer and with more depth, and they are less intense than the JH11s I have (which are great but kind of intense for want of a better work).  

My only slight problem is that the nozzle outlets are small and getting the wax out is really tricky without poking it in further.

target_product_title: Mockingbird Reference Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Legacy W500 mapped to current Mockingbird UIEM and CIEM per AAW request.
title: Custom process is not OK...
body:
I bought a pair of AAW W500 late 2017 and could never use it due to catastrophic fit. I don't know where it went wrong: myself, the audiologist (prints checked by photos by AAW before buying, I had to go to a second audiologist) or AAW. But in my opinion, the whole process is unsound: when the fit is not correct, it is incredibly difficult for the final user to guess what's exactly wrong. Do we need no remove some thickness here, or to add some thickness here? It turns out to be a "guess game", with a lot of time and money loss for both parties. (Sendind the earbuds to AAW would cost me about 50EUR (60USD) each time, plus there is a risk of tax or parcel loss). Finally, the process was so time consuming and, to avoid additional money loss, I decided not to return the earbuds for re-fit. So I have been having 1000EUR earbuds taking dust on my desk for more than a year. That's, by far, the most disappointing audio buy I ever made...

PS: re-post because my review was just deleted!

target_product_title: Z05 AuAg Launch Edition
mapping_note: Legacy AXH custom mapped to Z05 AuAg as the successor product per AAW request.
title: Okay, but not great for the price.
body:
I've had these AXH CIEM's for over a month now so I have a feel for them. Two of my band members bought CIEM's from the US and I thought I would follow suit; but I chose AAW because I thought their 'Dynamic Driver' technology was more advanced and better suited to my listening style (Website quote: "AXH is tuned with a balanced sound signature in mind, it offers controlled yet natural sub bass which you will never find in a full balanced armature setup"). and they were described as being 'tuned' flatter plus they came with the Lune Mk5 cable.
The build quality is very high and the Lune Mk5 cable is very good. Some have said it's heavy, but I personally don't think so. Separate to the product quality, the fit of my in-ears into my ears seems a touch loose. I suspect it's because AAW took so long (11 weeks all up) to use my in-ear moulds that they shrank (dehydrated, dried up).
I play bass in a band; don't buy these in-ear's if you want to use them professionally on stage as a bass player. They get a pass mark as being adequate, but not great! I also do long motorbike rides and I listen to primarily rock music during those rides. Clarity is excellent but they are harsh in the top frequencies. I also bought a Capri USB-C cable with an in-line Hi-Res DAC to plug into my HTC U12+. The volume is only adequately loud enough. When I ride, I plug the Lune Mk5 3.5mm cable into a FIIO BTR-3 Hi-Fi amp and bluetooth it to my HTC. This works very well with very high volume and excellent clarity, but the AXH's are always harsh at high volume.
I am able to compare the AXH directly with Shure SE535 and HTC's generic supplied ear buds. AXH is easily the clearest sound but overall I can't pick the winner when plugged into my HTC and they are not worth SGD$550 more than the HTC ear buds which understandably sound really good in an HTC phone. BTW, I'm only comparing sound quality here, nothing else.
AAW have a 30 day or sometimes 60 day refit policy. You need to read the fine pri...

target_product_title: POLA Electrostatic Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Only a live POLA universal product exists; not duplicated to custom.
title: Pola's
body:
I've only had these a few days so these are very initial impressions.
I use these with a Hiby R6 pro out of the 4.4 balanced port with the (very nice) Hakone cable.
They have a rather odd, boxy shape that will definitely stick out from your ears. 
Everyone's ears are shaped differently obviously and I could not get any workable fit/seal with any of the silicone tips. For me, the large foam tips work fine though and give me a comfortable, secure fit. I definitely don't think anyone would want to use these for working out/jogging etc..
   I'm not really an audiophile in the sense of accurately describing slight differences in timbre and fine details. I do have however several earphones in this relative price range and I know what sounds "good" overall, at least to me.These are very capable overall and sound better than my CA Jupiters, Vegas and my JHA custom 13somethings. 
   These have  OUTSTANDING bass. If bass is your thing, look no further, you're done.
I'm sure thats from the insane 13m  Dynamic Driver. At first I thought they were rather "muddy" due to the bass but the rest of the sound range seems to be opening up nicely with 
more use.
  I rate them a strong 4 stars but not 5 simply because I also own RHA CL2's and they can't 
match those but I'm not sure anything (at any price) can. The RHA's are just cleaner, tighter and faster. Perhaps with more use the Pola's will scale up. I'm definitely not disappointed with these however.
     I also want to say the service from Null Audio was top notch and I can't recommend them highly enough. Su answered my MANY questions promptly and was super helpful at every step. Many thanks!

title: Excellent IEM
body:
I purchased the Project 4+2 and am very pleased with the IEMs. The audio is natural neither warm or cool. The separation is exceptional. It expands into the existing surround sound by allowing sounds to be interpreted as three-dimensional with neither horizontal, nor vertical limitation.
It comes with 3 sound customizable sound filters (normal, Vokal, and bass)
The bass is powerful but not overwhelming nor does interfere with the mids or vocals. It completes the audio making the audio sound full without fatiguing your senses.
This IEM produces accurate sound allowing the audio/music to be heard as the producer intended. If you want an accurate IEM this is the IEM to go with.

target_product_title: A3H+ Lux Edition Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: Hidden Gems
body:
A3H+ Lux CIEM was an excellent choice in that price range. These are  hidden gems, and can out perform some of the well know brands. The custom shell and fit took approximately 8 weeks to complete, and around 4 days to receive in the USA. 
 The A3H+ Lux sound  can bounce between 'neutral/cold' and 'neutral/warm' depending on the media you are listening to. It has an impressive low end, crisp vocals/mids and highs.  All detailed and articulate. 
 The bass is punchy with emphasis on the mid bass as opposed to Sub Bass/Deep Bass (IMO). The bass hits nicely and powerful.  it's crisp and accentuates the track without mudding the mids or vocals. 
The soundstage is airy and a bit narrow, which for me is perfect. I find that too much air can  result in a hollow sounding experience with some distortion. The vocals are clear and detailed.  I would say that the 
 A3H+ Lux has a V shaped sound signature, balanced and accurate. 
These have a spectacular three-dimensional  sense of separation. Meaning... you can hear every instrument being played as it moves all around you while separate from the mids/vocals. 
 The A3H+ Lux also comes with 4 signature sound settings that you can adjust to your liking.  (Vocal, Normal, Vocal +, and Bass)
*This IEM does not do well with bass boosted tracks.  If you prefer majority of bass boosted music, these aren't the ears for you.  
 The A3H+ Lux  is a versatile IEM.. meaning - (depending on your DAPs power/ and settings) you can tweak the vocals by moving them to the front of the soundstage or bring the vocals to the middle of the soundstage and let the music encase the vocals.
 *CONS -
SubBass /Mid Bass is great, but I wish they would hit a little harder in the deep bass range. I have yet to find an IEM that can successfully pull it off. 
A side note to AAW- please make an IEM that can render rumbling Deep bass while maintaining that wonderful upper/mids/vocal clarity.

target_product_title: Mockingbird Reference Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Legacy Mockingbird handle mapped to current Mockingbird UIEM and CIEM.
title: Audio library rediscovery ahead !
body:
Hello,
The combination Mockingbird iem + Aoraki cable is really enjoyable, i.e. mind-blowing! Known tracks of various genres (baroque, reggae, trance, ...) are now more detailed. This gain doesn't come at the cost of musicality; you can enjoy beautifully rendered tunes as a whole or focus effortlessly on a counter melody!
I haven't tried them with my phone yet but they are easily driven by an Hiby R6 balanced output, so there shouldn't be any problem.
The only "warning" I would like to share is that these iem are thick (for a good reason, 1 dynamic driver + 2 mid BA + 2 high BA take some space). I own the universal version and they tend to get out of my ears when I get agitated. My advice would be to get the custom version to enhance insertion into the ears.
Anyway this is nothing compared to the pure audio bliss these iem represent!

Anyway this is nothing compared to the pure audio bliss these iem represent!

target_product_title: ASH Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: Excellent sound for those who want detail
body: These earphones are very different from the previous ones I had (triple fi 10 with extra driver turned into customs). The bass is definitely there and sounds really good but it isn't over-emphasized. As usual in this world of people interested in audio, it is hard to come up with the words to describe audio... so I don't mean to sound pretentious or to throw random adjectives around, but I will just describe the sound of these IEMs as "very high-quality, precise, and refined without being showy." In other words, where the triple fi 10 gave you plenty of bass and you went "wow!" but then realized that the bass was unrealistic and muffled other elements of the song, ASH doesn't blow your mind at first but then you find yourself smiling or closing your eyes to enjoy the great presentation of high quality sounds all over the spectrum. Drums especially sound fantastic. The combination of the hi-hat and the bass drum is just really really cool! It demonstrates that ASH can present lows and highs without shyness and keeping the quality. Granted, there are some songs in which the sharpness of the cymbals may be a little too intense. But overall, these are really enjoyable earphones and you know for sure they have great quality capable of revealing everything about your recordings and audio player. Feed them good sources on a good player and you will surely get to know your music in a more profound, intimate, and clear way, give them low-quality content and you will surely feel the need to get better files. Highly recommended!

target_product_title: A3H+ Lux Edition Universal In-Ear Monitor
mapping_note: Bundled matching UIEM/CIEM live product handles.
title: A3H+ Lux CIEM
body:
I had a chance to demo the unit along with some of their other offerings at their office, and the A3H+ Lux was the best sound quality to price for me.
I got my impressions done in their office, which was very quick and easy. 
They took about 9 weeks from purchase date to when I got them at my doorstep in the US. (~8 weeks to make, then 5 days to ship to my doorstep)
I use them in a live stage setting, and the fit and seal of a custom mold made it a much better experience compared to my universals in ear monitors.
Having the ability to change sound signatures with the small switches on the IEM was a big positive.
I got the carbon fiber faceplates and they look great! 
Overall, these are great CIEMs for those who are just getting into custom in ears monitors, and can't set aside the budget for the larger name brands or the higher end models from AAW.

title: Wonderful Service, top notch craftsmanship
body:
This was my first time using Null-Audio/AAW for their reshelling service, and I couldn't be happier with the results.

Null-Audio and AAW did an awesome job on reshelling a pair of used JH Audio JH13v2's to fit my own ears.  With 8 drivers per side and my own small ear impressions to work with, AAW had difficult task.  They went through several iterations to make sure both the sound signature and the cosmetic look of my earphones were perfect before they put their names on the final product.  The result was spectacular:  precious little pieces of art that fit my ears perfectly and sound sublime.  Hats off to both Null Audio and AAW for their technical expertise, their craftsmanship, and their high standards.

target_product_title: Earphone Custom Reshell Service by AAW
mapping_note: Null Audio reshell service reviews mapped to AAW's live reshell service product.
title: UE TF10 reshell
body: I sent my UE TF10 IEMs to Null Audio for a reshell service along with my in ear moulds, and received them back withing about 8 weeks. Communication with Null was good via email. I was not expecting such a high quality package. The parcel contained a high quality package, with all the contents in a material carry case. The case contained the left & right IEMs, a black twisted audio cable, carry pouch, microfibre cloth, airplane adapter, 6.3mm socket adapter, cleaning brush, and a AAW membership card. The IEMs themselves have an exceptional finish, and fit well. The sound quality is great so far. I use them in 2 configurations. Wired via Dragonfly Red usb DAC with my Pixel 4 XL phone, with USB Audio Player PRO app, which provides upto 24bit lossless playback of FLAC files. Sound quality is excellent here with great sound stage separation, bass response is punchy, strong mids, clear highs. Isolation is very good as expected, as the custom moulded shell fits exactly into each ear perfectly. I also use in a wireless configuration, with the Fostex TM-2 true wireless kit. The sound is not quite as good, as the max codec is AptX which is 16bit 356kbps playback. It's good enough though, for the portability, it offers great flexibility in many scenarios. Overall I am very pleased with the service and end product, I would highly recommend this to anyone who has a higher end universal IEM, to take your earphone to another level.

title: A 10BA masterpiece...
body:
Sent a pair of UM Mentor V1 (10 BA) to AAW for a reshell including a good impression of my ears, which are very small. Knowing it would take some time to get 10BAs into the small shell.
After 2 months of waiting I received the disptach note. Took FedEx 2 days from SG to Germany.
Unboxing the new CIEMs wasn´t anything special. They sent it well packaged but kept the packet very simple. Nothing to be sad about, since it´s only a reshell.
Holding and taking a closer look at them made me smile. Very well crafted with no impurity. The nozzle is long and has a smooth look. At the top of it are 3 precisely placed sound bores. So there is no cosmetic complaint. Moving on to how they fit.
The Fit:
Plugged them in and instantly felt a difference to any other universal IEM, even though some already had a deep fit. Each and every noisy surrounding weakened. I couldn´t believe how isolating CIEMs actually are. It brought a smile up on my face which pleased me for quiet some time. Went to different objects and knocked at them to hear and feel the isolation. Just prefect! But when I looked into the mirror it deluded my mind...
The left shell seemed to have a perfect fit compared to the other one. Sadly I rather spent the attention to how things looked, hence why it felt wrong at first. Also the fact that I had never listened to an IEM with more than 3 drivers made the impressions even worse. I didn´t know, how many songs in my library should sound like, in terms of instrumental separation, and balance between L/R. 
So I laid down on the sofa and gave it some time, switching through my genres.
Who would have guessed that all the senses can take such a huge part on how you sense the fit of an InEar.
It felt more and more right and more comfortable, the more I listened to them and had different songs played.
Now, the other day, I woke up and plugged them in again and didn´t feel much of a difference between L/R. Still, the right side felt it doesn´t have the same fit as the other ...

title: A3H New version (2018) - Incredible!
body:
After using and loving my A3HproV2 for some time i saw that AAW was offering a revised version of the A3H specially tunned for even better clarity and extension. I'm particularly sensitive to sibilance so I was a bit hesitant at first but Kevin was very patient and explained to me how the newer tuning for the A3H is very special and keeps the sound signature in the balanced yet detailed side. So I decided to go for the upgrade. 
I sent in new ear impressions. My new A3H took 8 weeks to arrive and Null Audio guys were always kind and patient attending all my questions. 
AAW packaging is beautiful, containing all accessories in a great and elegant box. Personalized card with my name was a nice touch. Very professional. 
Sound quality: absolutely fantastic. It's really amazing how these monitors can bring so much sound quality and dimension. Sound signature is perfectly balanced, clear and detailed highs that are non fatiguing without a single hint of veil on them. Mids are natural, detailed and revealing with realistic timbre. Brass, guitars, strings, drums, pianos and vocals sound real and uncolored. AAW tuning on the armature drivers for mids and highs is incredible. 
The propietary 10mm dynamic driver takes care of the lows. Tons of quality and rumble, going down to 20hz with ease and zero distortion. Lows are perfectly mixed with the mids and highs. No phase shifts here. AAW truexross electronics make wonders bringing a full and coherent sound that's dynamic, with lots of headroom and transparent presentation. The best sounding custom IEM I've ever tried, hands down. 
Fit: snug and precise. Excellent fit.
Build quality: 5/5 excellent. 
Cable: even when I've been using and loving the brevity S, I had the chance to compare the ethos cable with my newer Arete cable. I personally find Arete is the best cable in all aspects. Soundstage and tonality are greatly improved. Arete is a rugged cable and is very comfortable. Second place goes for the brevity S. Ethos cable...

title: Good quality re-she’ll with no defects in craftsmanship
body:
High quality Re-shell, unfortunately, right ear seal is not perfect. 
Another, more serious issue is sound signature: bassy signature that my universal w300 had is gone. I have a set of 1964 Ears v3, which was less boomy compared to w300 before re-she’ll, whereas now it’s vice-versa. My intention was to use w300 as workout IEMs, so extra base would be nice. 
During the re-shell, I was told that one of the drivers in the earphone was defective and needed to be replaced for extra $. I proceeded and paid extra, however when I was sending my earphones, everything worked well and there was no variance between the outputs from each of the earpieces (yes, I test them every 2-3 months to make sure that everything works fine).

target_product_title: Earphone Custom Reshell Service by AAW
mapping_note: Null Audio reshell service reviews mapped to AAW's live reshell service product.
title: JH Audio Roxanne Reshell Gone Wrong
body:
After waiting for 8 weeks, the reshell was done wrong, not according to my requirement. This is despite bringing my existing CIEM to show the insertion depth that I request, and marking the length on the ear impression. My ear requires the nozzle slightly passed 2nd bend, but the lab cut it short right at the 2nd bend. This causes the bores direction to face my ear drum off center, treble suffers. There is nothing wrong with the seal but rather, direction of the bores. I have to press very firmly at an angle into my ears in order to hear the clear treble. I have exact same UIEM Roxanne and did a A/B comparison, immediately it became very obvious. 

Another mistake done was the tube lengths for JH freqphase, Bass drivers shortest, mid drivers and high drivers longest. End up mid drivers was made shortest, Bass drivers in the middle, and high drivers was way too long all the way to the back. Messed up the whole freqphase! 
I was told a frequency response testing was done before and after, and there’s no change. But I doubt so, the sound signature sounds messed up. 

Went back for refit but I doubt they will grant to remake the shells again. Explained the freqphase error but they don’t seems to understand, gave up on this issue, most importantly is the shell corrected. 
I’ve pass my existing CIEM together back to lab, hope to hear good news back and will update this review.

Fit was perfect, but the acrylic shell wasn't molded well on one side. The speaker canals weren't fully covered, so the centre part is just exposed rubber tubing I can prod with a fingernail. Sent it back for repair, and got it back 3 or 4 weeks later with what looked like varnish over the exposed part. Asked the staff what it was, and she said it was the same material as the rest of the shell. So I took her word for it and left it at that.

About a week later, I'm cleaning it again, and the repair flaked off. It was just varnish after all. Honestly, a small defect like this would be fine if it was a $100+ item, but it cost me $300+. I've decided not to send it in for any further work since it's just going to be varnish, but I'm very disappointed.

title: Re-shell and Modify UE7
body: I sent my UE7 in because I wanted a new look for my IEMs. I wanted null to add one driver to the existing 3. The I got them back about two months later, I open the box and they were beautiful. They look and fit perfectly. No question these do great job. The one issue I have is that they took it upon themselves to make a decision they should have left up to me the customer. Instead of adding one driver to the existing three, they replaced the one high driver with one dual high driver. At first glance I thought that they didn't add any driver because the dual high driver was so small, it looked like the original single high driver. After I complained about what I though was an error on their part, they explained what they did. I told them they should have told me about this before they did it. I may have opted to not purchase extra high driver and just go for the re-shell alone. In any event, they sound good but they sounded good before the re-shell. So I may have paid for something that really didn't make a difference. I'll never know.

mapping_note: Null Audio reshell service reviews mapped to AAW's live reshell service product.
title: New life for Noble K10s - I wish I had done this a while ago!
body:
I love my Nobles, but after a few years the universal shells (as beautiful as they are) were looking pretty shabby.  I also had long noticed that, for my ears at least, bass response was significantly stronger when I pressed on the IEMs with a finger, as if the shells were radiating much of the power into the ether.  This was NOT a function of the seal. I did not experience this, for example, with my Fiio FH7s with the same tips.  

The work by AAW was first rate.  And, to my delight, the bass has been greatly improved.  No need to hold the IEMs firmly in place to get rumble and impact.  

My only minor issue is that the acrylic used has a significantly redder tint than expected.  Perhaps just a function of my monitor's color temperature.    See the pics below - the ones I received (cabled) and the mock-up from NA's site.  Still, 5 star product and 5 star service.

title: Better late than never
body:
Prior to writing this review for my third IEM order, I've had one custom order (ASH) and one re-shell. All my orders are of impeccable quality and sound great. My only complaint is that NA didn't communicate with me as I think they should have. I sent them my re-shell order on September 26 and did not receive any acknowledgment. On December 19 I sent an email inquiring about the status of my order. On January 6th I sent a second email inquiry.   
On Jan 6th I received a reply that my order would be shipped by the weekend. I received my order on January 12th.
NA has a great product. I only wished that they would practice better communication with the clients.

title: Reshelling custom in ear
body: Had some custom in ears that I had reshelled and they did a fabulous job. You have to have patience because it takes awhile. Takes between 2-3 months to get them back, but i'm very happy.

title: SE846 Reshell: WOW
body: I'm not one to write reviews, but felt the need for this. Sent my SE846 for a reshell (not custom). They had the Trishd mod and sounded fantastic. Now, they sound even better... Blowing my best expectations out of the water. Folks, don't hesitate.
